oligos.name: Preamp Oligo (oligo 1)
oligos.role: First-strand random-priming oligo carrying the P5-side Illumina adaptor (TruSeq Read 1); a 3' random hexamer anneals genome-wide to bisulfite-converted single-stranded DNA
oligos.notes: 5' segment CTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT is the TruSeq/PE Read 1 adaptor; 3' NNNNNN is a random hexamer (25% each base). Updated protocol uses hexamers; the original scBS-seq (Smallwood 2014) used a biotinylated nonamer version (see oligo_1_original).

oligos.name: Adapter 2 Oligo (oligo 2)
oligos.role: Second-strand random-priming oligo carrying the P7-side adaptor; a 3' random hexamer primes the first-strand product to add the second adaptor
oligos.notes: 5' segment TGCTGAACCGCTCTTCCGATCT is the second (P7-side) adaptor; 3' NNNNNN random hexamer. Original scBS-seq used a nonamer version (see oligo_2_original).

oligos.name: PE1.0
oligos.role: Forward library-amplification PCR primer; extends the adaptor-1 tag to the full P5 flow-cell sequence
oligos.notes: Illumina P5 forward primer. The 3' end (…TACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT) overlaps the Preamp Oligo adaptor. Final T is a phosphorothioate bond (denoted * in the protocol).

oligos.name: iPCRTag (indexed reverse primer)
oligos.role: Reverse library-amplification PCR primer; adds the P7 flow-cell sequence and an 8-bp sample index onto the adaptor-2 tag

components.name: i7 index
components.role: 8-bp sample/cell index (48 variants in Supplementary Table 1)

components.name: linker
components.role: spacer read by the custom iTag sequencing primer

components.name: adapter_2_anneal
components.role: anneals to the Adapter 2 tag; final T phosphorothioate

oligos.notes: 48 indexed primers (iPCRtag 1-48). Index read = reverse-complement of the 8-bp XXXXXXXX portion. Examples: iPCRtag1 index AACGTGAT -> read ATCACGTT; iPCRtag2 AAACATCG -> CGATGTTT; iPCRtag3 ATGCCTAA -> TTAGGCAT; iPCRtag4 AGTGGTCA -> TGACCACT.

oligos.name: iTag sequencing primer
oligos.role: Custom index-read sequencing primer used to read the 8-bp iPCRTag index
oligos.notes: Anneals within the adaptor-2/iPCRTag region and reads the index. Required when pooling libraries; standard Illumina i7 index primer does not sit at this position.

oligos.name: Oligo 1 (original scBS-seq, biotinylated nonamer)
oligos.role: First-strand random-priming oligo with 5' biotin and a 3' random nonamer (original Smallwood 2014 method)
oligos.notes: Written as (Biotin)CTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCTNNNNNNNNN. 5' biotin enabled streptavidin capture; the updated protocol replaced capture with SPRI and uses a hexamer (see preamp_oligo).

oligos.name: Oligo 2 (original scBS-seq, nonamer)
oligos.role: Second-strand random-priming oligo with a 3' random nonamer (original Smallwood 2014 method)
oligos.notes: Nonamer version of the adaptor-2 oligo used in the original scBS-seq publication.

final_library.source_label: scBS-seq final PBAT library (Clark et al. 2017 Nat Protoc; Smallwood et al. 2014 Nat Methods)
final_library.annotated_library_sequence: P5[AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC]-Read1/TruSeq[TCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT]-[SPACER:6 random hexamer]-[CDNA bisulfite-converted genomic insert]-[SPACER:6 random hexamer]-Read2/Adapter2[AGATCGGAAGAGCGGTTCAGCAGGAATGCCGAGACCGATCTC]-[SAMPLE_INDEX:8]-P7[ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG]
annotation_lines: random hexamer    : [SPACER:6]  (random-priming site, trimmed in analysis)
index (i7)        : [SAMPLE_INDEX:8]  read by custom iTag sequencing primer

evidence.locator: scBS-seq final PBAT library (Clark et al. 2017 Nat Protoc; Smallwood et al. 2014 Nat Methods)

library_generation.title: Single-cell collection and lysis
library_generation.summary: A single cell is collected into RLT Plus buffer, lysing it and denaturing protein in one step.
library_generation.note: FACS or mouth-pipetting into 2.5 ul Buffer RLT Plus (guanidine thiocyanate + detergent); no proteinase K needed. Optional 60 fg unmethylated lambda DNA spike-in as a bisulfite-conversion control.

library_generation.title: Bisulfite conversion
library_generation.summary: Bisulfite treatment deaminates unmethylated C to U while fragmenting the DNA into single strands.
library_generation.note: Performed directly on lysate (Zymo/Imprint reagent). Methylated C is protected; unmethylated C -> U (read as T). DNA becomes single-stranded and fragmented.
library_generation.product: 5'- ...T G U T G U... (ssDNA, unmethyl-C -> U; 5mC retained) -3'

library_generation.title: First-strand synthesis / adaptor 1 tagging (x5)
library_generation.summary: Preamp Oligo random hexamers prime the ssDNA and Klenow exo- extends, adding adaptor 1; repeated five times with heat denaturation.
library_generation.note: Five rounds of random priming+extension capture each locus multiple times and add the P5-side adaptor at the 5' end of each new strand.
library_generation.product:
5'- CTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT NNNNNN -------------------->
                                          3'- ...converted genomic template... -5'
  product: 5'- CTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT NNNNNN [copied genomic] -3'

library_generation.title: Exonuclease I cleanup
library_generation.summary: Exonuclease I digests leftover single-stranded Preamp Oligos to prevent adaptor dimers.
library_generation.note: Removes unextended oligo 1 before adaptor-2 tagging.

library_generation.title: SPRI (AMPure XP) purification
library_generation.summary: First-strand products are purified with AMPure XP beads kept in-tube ('with-bead' method).
library_generation.note: Removes short fragments/oligos; beads retained for subsequent steps.

library_generation.title: Second-strand synthesis / adaptor 2 tagging
library_generation.summary: Adapter 2 Oligo random hexamers prime the first-strand product and Klenow extends, adding the P7-side adaptor.
library_generation.note: One round of random priming on the adaptor-1-tagged strands installs the second adaptor, producing double-adaptor-tagged molecules.
library_generation.product:
5'- CTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT NNNNNN [genomic] NNNNNN AGATCGGAAGAGCGGTTCAGCA -3'
3'- GATGTGCTGCGAGAAGGCTAGA NNNNNN [genomic] NNNNNN TGCTGAACCGCTCTTCCGATCT -5'

library_generation.title: PEG/SPRI purification of double-tagged products
library_generation.summary: Double-tagged fragments are purified with PEG buffer + AMPure XP beads.
library_generation.note: Size-selects away oligo concatemers before amplification.

library_generation.title: Indexed library amplification (PCR)
library_generation.summary: PE1.0 and an indexed iPCRTag primer amplify the fragments, completing P5, P7, and the 8-bp cell index.
library_generation.note: KAPA HiFi HotStart, ~10-14 cycles (13 typical for mouse). PE1.0 extends adaptor 1 to full P5; iPCRTag extends adaptor 2 to P7 + index.

library_generation.title: Final PEG/SPRI purification
library_generation.summary: The amplified library is purified and eluted, ready for QC and sequencing.
library_generation.note: Fragments >200 bp, mean 400-600 bp; quantified by KAPA qPCR (2-10 nM).

library_sequencing.note: 100-150 bp. Reads through the 6-nt random hexamer priming site (trimmed in analysis) then into the bisulfite-converted genomic insert, giving per-CpG methylation calls. Aligned single-end, non-directional.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 1 primer: 5'-ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT------------------------->  (reads →)
top:    5'-...ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT NNNNNN [BS-converted genomic insert] ...-3'
bottom: 3'-...TGTGAGAAAGGGATGTGCTGCGAGAAGGCTAGA NNNNNN [BS-converted genomic insert] ...-5'
                                            ↑ random hexamer (trim 6 nt)  → 5mC/CpG readout

library_sequencing.note: Reads the 8-bp iPCRTag sample/cell index (= reverse-complement of the primer's XXXXXXXX index portion) to demultiplex pooled single cells. Required only when libraries are pooled; standard i7 primer does not bind here.
library_sequencing.diagram:
iTag primer: 5'-AAGAGCGGTTCAGCAGGAATGCCGAGACCGATCTC------->  (reads →, 8 cyc)
top:    5'-...AAGAGCGGTTCAGCAGGAATGCCGAGACCGATCTC XXXXXXXX ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG-3'
bottom: 3'-...TTCTCGCCAAGTCGTCCTTACGGCTCTGGCTAGAG XXXXXXXX TAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC-5'
                                             ↑ 8-nt i7 index          ↑ P7

library_sequencing.primer: Standard Illumina Read 2 primer (binds the adapter-2 region)
library_sequencing.note: Paired-end read from the P7/adapter-2 side; first 6 bp random hexamer trimmed, remainder is bisulfite-converted genomic sequence. Even paired-end libraries are aligned single-end because PBAT generates hybrid fragments; R2 is merged with R1 for coverage.
library_sequencing.diagram:
top:    5'-...NNNNNN [BS-converted genomic insert] NNNNNN AGATCGGAAGAGCGGTTCAGCAGGAATGCCGAGACCGATCT [i7][P7]-3'
bottom: 3'-...NNNNNN [BS-converted genomic insert] NNNNNN TCTAGCCTTCTCGCCAAGTCGTCCTTACGGCTCTGGCTAGA [i7][P7]-5'
Read 2 primer:            (reads ← into insert)  <------------- 5'-AGATCGGAAGAGCGGTTCAGCAGGAATGCCGAGACCGATCT-3'
                          anneals to bottom strand; 3' end at hexamer junction, extends into insert

title: scBS-seq
description: scBS-seq (single-cell bisulfite sequencing) measures genome-wide DNA methylation (5mC) at single-CpG, base resolution in individual cells. It is a post-bisulfite adaptor tagging (PBAT) method: single cells are lysed and bisulfite-converted first (simultaneously fragmenting DNA and deaminating unmethylated cytosines to uracil/thymine), then Illumina adaptors are incorporated by five rounds of random-primed first-strand synthesis with an adaptor-1 oligo, followed by second-adaptor tagging and indexed PCR. Sequencing reads capture bisulfite-converted genomic sequence (methylation state at CpGs) flanked by random-priming sites, and an 8-bp iPCRTag index identifies each single cell. Libraries are nondirectional (OT:CTOT:CTOB:OB ≈ 2:3:3:2) and are sequenced single-end even when paired-end reads are collected.

reference.label: Genome-wide base-resolution mapping of DNA methylation in single cells using scBS-seq

original_publication.title: Genome-wide base-resolution mapping of DNA methylation in single cells using single-cell bisulfite sequencing (scBS-seq)

throughput.summary: One library per single cell; up to 96 cells processed in parallel (Agilent Bravo). Recovers 10-20% of CpGs at 10-20 M reads, rising to a maximum 48.4% (mean ~17.7%) of genomic CpGs at saturating depth in mouse cells.
throughput.cells: Single cells (1 cell/library); 12-20 cells per HiSeq lane; ~96 in parallel
throughput.dna: up to 10.1 million CpGs (48.4% of all CpGs) covered; mean 3.7 M CpGs per cell

publication.statistical_model: Per-window methylation modeled as a Binomial random variable with a Beta(1,1) prior (Bayesian maximum a posteriori rate estimate); mean methylation across cells modeled as a Gaussian with weighted maximum-likelihood mean and unbiased weighted (chi-squared CI) cell-to-cell variance.

other.label: BS conversion efficiency
other.value: ≥97.7% (≥98.5% by unmethylated mitochondrial chromosome); QC excludes libraries with CHH methylation >5%

other.label: Model system
other.value: Mouse E14 ESCs (2i vs serum) and MII oocytes (C57BL/6Babr); GEO accession GSE56879

data_processing.summary: After the sequencing facility demultiplexes the 8-bp iPCRTag index, each single-cell library is processed independently: reads are QC'd, the 6-nt random-priming segment and read-through adapters are trimmed, and reads are aligned single-end in non-directional (PBAT) mode to a reference genome, deduplicated, and per-cytosine methylation calls extracted to coverage/bedGraph files.
